channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-25
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects, with notable updates in **scienceverse/metacheck**, **giladfeldman/docpluck**, and **Davidvandijcke/coarse**. The **scienceverse/metacheck** project has improved its navigation and home page, while also addressing a critical "coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure" and merging several pull requests. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** has added important documentation and fixes for tooling and tables, showcasing the project's growth. Additionally, **Davidvandijcke/coarse** has made strides in localization and deployment, with updates to its 'main' and 'dev' branches. These connections demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, with projects like **JamieCummins/regcheck** and **giladfeldman/escicheck** also making notable progress, including enhancements to the command-line interface and syncing with external repositories. Overall, it's an exciting time for our community, with many projects pushing forward and building on each other's successes.

66 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck

Most active:

docpluck17 commits
regcheck17 commits
metacheck15 commits
coarse15 commits
escicheck2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 8 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The main branch saw changes to the home page and navigation bar, with the text on the home page being updated to "Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual" and the navbar being modified to replace "Get Started/Reference/Articles" with a "Metacheck Manual link". On the dev branch, a fix was applied to address the "coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure", and a new file "R/import-papers.R" was added to ensure that dev installs cleanly. The dev branch also saw the merger of several pull requests, including "#317 from scienceverse/papers_databases", "#316 from scienceverse/ethics_check", "#309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck", and "#318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist", which were merged by Lisa DeBruine. Both the main and dev branches were worked on in these commits.

8 commit(s) today:

  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch saw two commits, with one adding documentation for the 2026-06-25 run, including handoff, learnings, and project lessons, as noted in the commit message "docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL)". The `main` branch also received a fix for tooling, which added a direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note and included RC-T/RC-1 spec findings, as stated in the commit message "fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings". The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch had three commits, including one that added documentation for learnings on RC-T Layer-2 and combined-ship closeout, as described in the commit message "docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97)". This branch also saw a fix for tables, specifically addressing issues with DP-2 and DP-5, including blank p/df and two-header-row recovery, as well as RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress, as noted in the commit message "fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress". Additionally, the `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch received a fix for rendering, which stripped Camelot tables absorbed by body prose in RC-T, as stated in the commit message "fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96)".

2 commit(s) today:

  • 976af30 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL) — Gilad Feldman
  • 59cff5b [main] fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 3 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, where a "debug of CLI report" was performed. Additionally, on the staging branch, "further local LLM support in CLI" was added, enhancing the command-line interface's capabilities. The staging branch also received "UI fixes", which addressed issues with the user interface. The commits all occurred on the same day, indicating a concentrated effort to refine the project's functionality and usability on the staging branch.

3 commit(s) today:

  • b0d8ab2 [staging] debug of CLI report — Jamie Cummins
  • 97e8203 [staging] add further local LLM support in CLI — Jamie Cummins
  • df873ec [staging] UI fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: activity update
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit synced changes from `escicheckapp@bd6f007`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.5`, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the `main` branch, synced changes from `escicheckapp@dec37b1`, which is associated with `effectcheck v0.6.4`. These commits brought the `main` branch up to date with versions `v0.6.4` and `v0.6.5` of `effectcheck` from the `escicheckapp` repository. The commits were made in close succession, with both occurring on the same day, June 21, 2026.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
